meta.description: A two-dimensional sparse container — the grid is to tables what tree is to hierarchies.

columns.description: Column definitions. null means indexed mode — columns are auto-labelled A, B, C… (Excel-style) and unbounded; Studio renders columns as they are occupied. When set to an array, the grid is in named-column mode — only the declared columns exist; Studio renders them in declaration order with their labels and optional type hints. Mixed mode is not supported: a grid is either fully indexed or fully named.

key.description: Internal identifier for this column. Used in cell.payload.column to reference the column. Must be unique within this grid. Stable — changing a column key orphans existing cell items that reference the old key.

label.description: Display name shown in the column header in Studio.

type.description: Optional type hint for Studio rendering and input form generation. Known values: 'string' (text input), 'number' (numeric input), 'boolean' (checkbox), 'date' (date picker), 'uuid' (UUID picker — links to another item), 'formula' (formula expression — Studio evaluates and renders the result). Open string — new type hints do not require a spec change. Null means no type constraint; Studio renders the cell value as plain text.

width.description: Display width hint in pixels. Null means Studio chooses a default. Informational — the grid component may ignore this on small viewports.

rowCount.description: Display hint: how many rows Studio renders initially before the user scrolls or expands. Does not cap how many rows can be occupied — a cell with row=9999 is valid regardless of rowCount. Null means Studio renders until the last occupied row plus a small buffer. In indexed mode (columns: null), this is the only way to define the visible grid size before any cells are filled.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this grid contains and how it should be used. Shown in Studio alongside the grid label.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
